oracle_candidates.name: won_info_field
oracle_candidates.usable_reason: Primary official evaluator signal: direct API access, deterministic, exact binary PDDL-goal check used by evaluate_dagger.py to compute success_rate and average_game_points.
oracle_candidates.misuse_risk: Using won as a step-level dense reward signal rather than an episode-level binary label produces a misleading sparse signal that fires only on the final winning step.

oracle_candidates.name: score_info_field
oracle_candidates.usable_reason: Official score field (float) returned by evaluate_dagger.py; numerically identical to won cast to float, supports average_game_points metric.
oracle_candidates.misuse_risk: Treating score as a continuous signal is misleading since it is binary (0.0 or 1.0); does not provide additional resolution beyond won_info_field.

oracle_candidates.name: pddl_facts_state
oracle_candidates.usable_reason: Exposes complete symbolic world state as List[Proposition]; provides deterministic per-step predicates (holds, inreceptacle, opened, toggled) required to verify per-candidate trigger conditions for C02–C05.
oracle_candidates.misuse_risk: PDDL entity names use internal IDs unless AlfredDemangler is applied; comparing facts against human-readable names without demangling will fail silently.

oracle_candidates.name: policy_commands_plan
oracle_candidates.usable_reason: Leaks the PDDL-planner's solution path directly; providing or inspecting this field in any test context defeats the benchmark's challenge and violates non-leakage requirements.
oracle_candidates.misuse_risk: Directly exposes the optimal action sequence from current state; use in any test would constitute solution leakage and invalidate benchmark integrity.

oracle_candidates.name: expert_plan_handcoded
oracle_candidates.usable_reason: Rejected: may raise HandCodedAgentTimeout on complex states and falls back to 'look', making it an unreliable oracle; pddl_facts_state provides equivalent or better diagnostic coverage without timeout risk.
oracle_candidates.misuse_risk: Expert may return 'look' as fallback when it cannot determine next action; treating 'look' responses as always-valid oracle signals produces false negatives.

oracle_candidates.name: traj_data_task_annotation
oracle_candidates.usable_reason: Rejected: requires fragile path joining via extra.gamefile info field; pddl_facts_state subsumes its state-checking role without external file I/O.
oracle_candidates.misuse_risk: Path joining logic is fragile if data is relocated; correlation with extra.gamefile must be exact or silent mismatches occur.

oracle_candidates.name: admissible_commands_validity_heuristic
oracle_candidates.usable_reason: Enumerates all currently PDDL-executable actions; provides deterministic action-validity binary oracle for C01 trigger verification and Layer 3 admissible_commands non-regression checks.
oracle_candidates.misuse_risk: Membership in admissible_commands is necessary but not sufficient for goal progress; must not be used as a sole task-completion oracle.

oracle_candidates.name: goal_condition_success_rate_thor
oracle_candidates.usable_reason: Rejected: only available in AlfredThorEnv (visual simulator) path; not exposed in the text-only AlfredTWEnv path that is the primary ALFWorld interface used by this benchmark.
oracle_candidates.misuse_risk: Silently returns null or raises AttributeError in text-only AlfredTWEnv path; using it as an oracle in text-mode tests produces vacuously passing checks.

convenience_oracle_justifications.why_used_without_official_equivalent: The official oracle (won_info_field) only signals episode-terminal success and cannot verify per-step precondition predicates (holds, inreceptacle, opened, toggled) required to confirm C02–C05 trigger and non-leakage conditions at sub-episode granularity.

The official oracle cannot confirm per-step action-space invariants; admissible_commands is the only deterministic source that distinguishes a completely-invalid-verb command (C01) from other Nothing-happens causes, and is the only mechanism for Layer 3 admissible_commands field non-regression verification.

rejected_oracles.name: policy_commands_plan
rejected_oracles.reason: Directly exposes the optimal solution path; any test using this oracle would constitute solution leakage and violate Rule 4 non-leakage requirements.

rejected_oracles.name: expert_plan_handcoded
rejected_oracles.reason: HandCodedAgentTimeout risk and 'look' fallback behavior make it an unreliable oracle; pddl_facts_state provides strictly superior diagnostic coverage.

rejected_oracles.name: traj_data_task_annotation
rejected_oracles.reason: Requires fragile external file I/O via extra.gamefile path joining; pddl_facts_state subsumes all state-checking needs at runtime without filesystem dependencies.

rejected_oracles.name: goal_condition_success_rate_thor
rejected_oracles.reason: Only available in AlfredThorEnv (visual simulator) path; not usable in text-only AlfredTWEnv benchmark configuration.

rejected_oracles.name: score_info_field
rejected_oracles.reason: Numerically identical to won_info_field (binary 0.0/1.0); no additional information beyond the primary official oracle; including it as a separate chosen oracle would be redundant.
